Reentries.title: Reentries

toolbar.flyToCorridorTooltip: When enabled, clicking a TIP row flies the camera to the predicted reentry corridor on the globe instead of following the object.

help.overview: The Reentries menu tracks objects falling out of orbit. It has two tabs: official reentry predictions from US Space Command, and a live analysis of the lowest objects in the catalog.
help.imgAlt: The Reentries menu showing the TIP Messages table
help.imgCaption: TIP reentry predictions. Clicking a row jumps the simulation to the predicted decay time.
help.tabs:
• TIP Messages: Tracking and Impact Prediction messages published by US Space Command, with the predicted decay time, location, uncertainty window (minutes), reentry angle, RCS, and mass. Clicking a row selects the object and jumps the simulation time to the predicted decay epoch.
  • Reentry Analysis: roughly 200 cataloged objects sorted by lowest perigee. Rows are color coded: red means perigee below 120 km (reentry imminent), yellow means 120–160 km (high risk). Clicking a row selects the object.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu from the bottom bar or press R.
  2. Click the download icon to fetch the latest TIP data (requires an internet connection).
  3. Click any prediction to fly to that object at its predicted decay time.
  4. Switch to the Reentry Analysis tab to browse what is decaying next. It loads the first time you open it.
help.tip1: A "Reentered" entry in the Mean Alt column means the object can no longer be propagated. It has most likely already come down.
help.tip2: TIP predictions carry real uncertainty: the Window column is the uncertainty in minutes, which can span continents of ground track.
help.tip3: Clicking a TIP row silently changes the simulation clock to the decay epoch. Use the time controls to come back to the present.

Reentries.dataSource: Tracking and Impact Prediction (TIP) messages provided by the US Space Command (USSPACECOM).
